response:
# Perspective Audit — Navigation Menu With Complete Landmarks ## Step 1 — Escalated Perspectives Confirmed From the a11y-critic escalation: - **Screen Reader & Semantic Structure — MEDIUM** → deep review (Perspective 6) Perspectives **not** escalated (LOW / not flagged — not reviewed per protocol): Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion, Auditory Access, Keyboard & Motor, Cognitive & Neurodivergent. Scope note: the artifact is a React **page-fragment component** (`NavMenuPage` returns a `
`, not a document). Criteria that live in the document `` or the routing layer — `` (2.4.2), `<html lang>` (3.1.1), and SPA route-change announcement (4.1.3) — cannot be satisfied or failed *inside* this component. They are recorded as human-verification boundaries, not as findings against the component. Manufacturing a barrier out of markup the component structurally cannot contain would be a false alarm on clean code. --- ## Step 4 — Screen Reader & Semantic Structure Checklist | # | Checklist item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | Landmark regions present and correctly used (1.3.1) | **PASS** | `<header>` L20, `<main id="main-content">` L42, `<aside aria-label="Related products">` L60, `<footer>` L72, four `<nav>` L22/33/62/73. All five landmark types present. | | 1a | Multiple same-type landmarks each uniquely named (1.3.1 / APG) | **PASS (with MINOR note)** | Four navs carry distinct labels: "Primary navigation" L22, "Breadcrumb" L33, "Related product links" L62, "Footer navigation" L73. Distinct and disambiguated. See Finding SR-1 for a label-wording refinement. | | 2 | Heading hierarchy logical, `<h1>` present, descriptive (2.4.6) | **PASS** | `<h1>Widget Pro</h1>` L43 → `<h2>` L46, L55 in `<main>`; `<h2>Related Products</h2>` L61 in `<aside>`. No skipped levels; every heading describes its content. | | 3 | Informative images have `alt`; decorative `alt=""` (1.1.1) | **N/A** | No `<img>` elements. The logo is a text link (`<a class="logo">Acme Corp</a>` L21), not an image. | | 4 | DOM/source order matches visual reading order (1.3.2) | **PASS** | `.content-layout` grid `1fr 280px` L181, media collapse to one column L187–189. `<main>` precedes `<aside>` in DOM (L42 before L60) and sits in the left/wide column — no `order`, `row-reverse`, or positioning that would desync source and visual order. | | 5 | Unique, descriptive `<title>` (2.4.2) | **BOUNDARY** | Not renderable from a fragment component; set at document/head level. `[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]` | | 6 | Link text describes destination in context (2.4.4) | **PASS** | Every link is self-describing: "Skip to main content" L18, nav items "Products/Solutions/Pricing/Documentation/Contact us" L24–28, breadcrumb crumbs L35–37, "See full pricing" L56, related-product names L65–67, footer legal links L75–77. No "click here" / "read more". | | 7 | `lang` on `<html>` matches page language (3.1.1) | **BOUNDARY** | `<html>` is outside the component. `[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]` | | 8 | Foreign-language phrases wrapped in `lang` (3.1.2) | **N/A** | All content is English; no foreign phrases. | | 9 | Every form input has associated visible `<label>` (3.3.2) | **N/A** | No form controls in this component. | | 10 | Error messages identify field, associated via `aria-describedby` (3.3.1) | **N/A** | No forms / no error states. | | 11 | Custom widgets use correct ARIA roles per APG (4.1.2) | **PASS** | No custom widgets — only native `nav/ul/ol/li/a/header/main/aside/footer`. Breadcrumb correctly follows the APG pattern: `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">` → `<ol>` → `aria-current="page"` on the terminal crumb (L33–38). `<section>`s named via `aria-labelledby` pointing at their `<h2>` (L45/46, L54/55) — correct association. | | 12 | Status messages use `aria-live` with correct urgency (4.1.3) | **N/A** | Static page; no dynamic status messages to announce. | | 13 | SPA route changes: title updates, focus managed, change announced | **BOUNDARY** | Links are plain `<a href>` (L24–28 etc.). Whether navigation is a full page load or client-side route change — and any accompanying title/focus/announcement handling — is not expressed in this component. `[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]` | **Red flags checked (all clear):** No missing `<main>` (present L42). No unlabeled form inputs (no forms). No custom widget lacking a role (none present). `<html lang>` red flag is not determinable from a fragment and is not asserted as fired. **Code-level vs content-level:** All applicable code-level items PASS. No content-level defects. Three items are scope boundaries requiring human verification at the document/routing layer. --- ## Step 5 — Findings **Finding:** SR-1 — Two of the four `<nav>` accessible names embed the word "navigation," which duplicates the landmark role the screen reader already announces. `aria-label="Primary navigation"` (L22) and `aria-label="Footer navigation"` (L73) are announced as roughly "Primary navigation, navigation landmark" / "Footer navigation, navigation landmark." The other two navs already avoid this — "Breadcrumb" (L33) and "Related product links" (L62) read cleanly — so the fixture is internally inconsistent as well as slightly verbose. Per the WAI-ARIA APG landmark-labeling convention, the role word should be omitted from the label because AT appends it. **WCAG:** 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (AA) — satisfied; this is an APG authoring refinement, not a success-criterion failure. **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MINOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author) **Evidence:** Fixture L22 `aria-label="Primary navigation"`, L73 `aria-label="Footer navigation"` (contrast with clean L33 "Breadcrumb", L62 "Related product links") **Fix:** Drop the redundant role word for consistency with the other two navs: `aria-label="Primary"` and `aria-label="Footer"` (or "Site footer"). Optional polish — does **not** block access, does not change the verdict, and the current labels are still distinct and fully usable. No CRITICAL, MAJOR, or ENHANCEMENT findings. --- ## Step 6 — Perspective Audit Summary **Component/artifact reviewed:** Navigation Menu With Complete Landmarks (`NavMenuPage`, CLEAN fixture) **Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (MEDIUM) **Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity, Auditory Access, Keyboard & Motor Access, Cognitive & Neurodivergent ### Findings by Severity | Severity | Count | |---|---| | CRITICAL | 0 | | MAJOR | 0 | | MINOR | 1 | | ENHANCEMENT | 0 | ### Perspectives That Passed - **Screen Reader & Semantic Structure** — All applicable checklist items PASS or N/A. Semantics are strong: complete and correctly nested landmarks, four uniquely-labeled navs, an APG-correct breadcrumb with `aria-current="page"`, `aria-labelledby`-named sections, a clean h1→h2 outline, fully descriptive link text, and DOM order that matches visual order. The single MINOR (SR-1) is a label-wording refinement, not a barrier. The MEDIUM escalation does not survive the deep review as a real access defect — this markup is genuinely clean. ### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role **Front-End Dev** - Redundant role word in two nav `aria-label`s — Screen Reader & Semantic Structure — MINOR — 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (APG labeling convention) **Visual Design** — none **UX Design** — none **Content Author** — none (secondary on SR-1 only) ### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification These sit outside the component fragment and must be confirmed at the document/app-shell/routing layer — they are boundaries, not findings: 1. **Page `<title>` (2.4.2)** — Confirm the app shell / head manager sets a unique, descriptive title (e.g., "Widget Pro — Acme Corp") for this route. 2. **`<html lang>` (3.1.1)** — Confirm the document root declares `lang="en"`. 3. **SPA route-change behavior (4.1.3, 2.4.2)** — If the header/breadcrumb/related links trigger client-side navigation, confirm the title updates, focus is moved sensibly (e.g., to `<h1>` or `<main>`), and the change is announced. If these are full page loads, this is moot. ### Recommended Next Step **PASS** — No CRITICAL or MAJOR findings. The one MINOR (SR-1) is optional polish that can ship as-is or be tidied opportunistically; it does not warrant REVISE. Resolve the three human-verification boundaries at the document/routing layer (they are not defects in this component). The a11y-critic's MEDIUM alarm for this perspective is **not confirmed** as a real barrier on deep review — treat the escalation as cleared.

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
